functions.name: dart_physics.mink.utils.move_mocap_to_frame
documentation:
Initialize mocap body pose at a desired frame.

Args:
    model: Mujoco model.
    data: Mujoco data.
    mocap_name: The name of the mocap body.
    frame_name: The desired frame name.
    frame_type: The desired frame type. Can be "body", "geom" or "site".

functions.name: dart_physics.mink.utils.get_freejoint_dims
documentation:
Get all floating joint configuration and tangent indices.

Args:
    model: Mujoco model.

Returns:
    A (q_ids, v_ids) pair containing all floating joint indices in the
    configuration and tangent spaces respectively.

functions.name: dart_physics.mink.utils.custom_configuration_vector
documentation:
Generate a configuration vector where named joints have specific values.

Args:
    model: Mujoco model.
    key_name: Optional keyframe name to initialize the configuration vector from.
        Otherwise, the default pose `qpos0` is used.
    kwargs: Custom values for joint coordinates.

Returns:
    Configuration vector where named joints have the values specified in
        keyword arguments, and other joints have their neutral value or value
        defined in the keyframe if provided.

functions.name: dart_physics.mink.utils.get_subtree_geom_ids
documentation:
Get all geoms belonging to subtree starting at a given body.

Args:
    model: Mujoco model.
    body_id: ID of body where subtree starts.

Returns:
    A list containing all subtree geom ids.

functions.name: dart_physics.mink.utils.get_body_geom_ids
documentation:
Get all geoms belonging to a given body.

Args:
    model: Mujoco model.
    body_id: ID of body.

Returns:
    A list containing all body geom ids.

modules.name: dart_physics.mink.configuration
documentation:
Configuration space of a robot model.

The {class}`Configuration` class encapsulates a MuJoCo
[model](https://mujoco.readthedocs.io/en/latest/APIreference/APItypes.html#mjmodel)
and [data](https://mujoco.readthedocs.io/en/latest/APIreference/APItypes.html#mjdata),
offering easy access to frame transforms and frame Jacobians. A frame refers to a coordinate
system that can be attached to various parts of the robot, such as a body, geom, or site.

classes.name: dart_physics.mink.configuration.Configuration

methods.name: dart_physics.mink.configuration.Configuration.__init__
documentation:
Constructor.

Args:
    model: Mujoco model.
    q: Configuration to initialize from. If None, the configuration
        is initialized to the default configuration `qpos0`.

methods.name: dart_physics.mink.configuration.Configuration.update
documentation:
Run forward kinematics.

Args:
    q: Optional configuration vector to override internal `data.qpos` with.

methods.name: dart_physics.mink.configuration.Configuration.update_from_keyframe
documentation:
Update the configuration from a keyframe.

Args:
    key_name: The name of the keyframe.

methods.name: dart_physics.mink.configuration.Configuration.check_limits
documentation:
Check that the current configuration is within bounds.

Args:
    tol: Tolerance in [rad].
    safety_break: If True, stop execution and raise an exception if the current
        configuration is outside limits. If False, print a warning and continue
        execution.

methods.name: dart_physics.mink.configuration.Configuration.get_frame_jacobian
documentation:
Compute the Jacobian matrix of a frame velocity.

Denoting our frame by $B$ and the world frame by $W$, the
Jacobian matrix ${}_B J_{WB}$ is related to the body velocity
${}_B v_{WB}$ by:

$$
{}_B v_{WB} = {}_B J_{WB} \dot{q}
$$

Args:

: frame_name: Name of the frame in the MJCF.
  frame_type: Type of frame. Can be a geom, a body or a site.

Returns:

: Jacobian ${}_B J_{WB}$ of the frame.

methods.name: dart_physics.mink.configuration.Configuration.get_transform_frame_to_world
documentation:
Get the pose of a frame at the current configuration.

Args:
    frame_name: Name of the frame in the MJCF.
    frame_type: Type of frame. Can be a geom, a body or a site.

Returns:
    The pose of the frame in the world frame.

methods.name: dart_physics.mink.configuration.Configuration.get_transform
documentation:
Get the pose of a frame with respect to another frame at the current
configuration.

Args:
    source_name: Name of the frame in the MJCF.
    source_type: Source type of frame. Can be a geom, a body or a site.
    dest_name: Name of the frame to get the pose in.
    dest_type: Dest type of frame. Can be a geom, a body or a site.

Returns:
    The pose of `source_name` in `dest_name`.

methods.name: dart_physics.mink.configuration.Configuration.integrate
documentation:
Integrate a velocity starting from the current configuration.

Args:
    velocity: The velocity in tangent space.
    dt: Integration duration in [s].

Returns:
    The new configuration after integration.

methods.name: dart_physics.mink.configuration.Configuration.integrate_inplace
documentation:
Integrate a velocity and update the current configuration inplace.

Args:
    velocity: The velocity in tangent space.
    dt: Integration duration in [s].

methods.name: dart_physics.mink.configuration.Configuration.q
documentation: The current configuration vector.
Aliases.

methods.name: dart_physics.mink.configuration.Configuration.nv
documentation: The dimension of the tangent space.

methods.name: dart_physics.mink.configuration.Configuration.nq
documentation: The dimension of the configuration space.

Encapsulates a model and data for convenient access to kinematic quantities.

This class provides methods to access and update the kinematic quantities of a robot
model, such as frame transforms and Jacobians. It performs forward kinematics at every
time step, ensuring up-to-date information about the robot's state.

Key functionalities include:

* Running forward kinematics to update the state.
* Checking configuration limits.
* Computing Jacobians for different frames.
* Retrieving frame transforms relative to the world frame.
* Integrating velocities to update configurations.

Args:

methods.name: dart_physics.mink.tasks.posture_task.PostureTask.compute_error
documentation:
Compute the posture task error.

The error is defined as:

$$
e(q) = q^* \ominus q
$$

Args:

: configuration: Robot configuration $q$.

Returns:

: Posture task error vector $e(q)$.

methods.name: dart_physics.mink.tasks.posture_task.PostureTask.compute_jacobian
documentation:
Compute the posture task Jacobian.

The task Jacobian is defined as:

$$
J(q) = I_{n_v}
$$

Args:

: configuration: Robot configuration $q$.

Returns:

: Posture task jacobian $J(q)$.

Regulate the joint angles of the robot towards a desired posture.

A posture is a vector of actuated joint angles. Floating-base coordinates are not
affected by this task.

Attributes:
    target_q: Target configuration.

methods.name: dart_physics.mink.tasks.relative_frame_task.RelativeFrameTask.set_target_from_configuration
documentation:
Set the target pose from a given robot configuration.

Args:

: configuration: Robot configuration $q$.

Regulate the pose of a frame relative to another frame.

Attributes:
    frame_name: Name of the frame to regulate, typically the name of body, geom
        or site in the robot model.
    frame_type: The frame type: `body`, `geom` or `site`.

    transform_target_to_root: Target pose in the root frame.

modules.name: dart_physics.mink.tasks.frame_task
documentation: Frame task implementation.

classes.name: dart_physics.mink.tasks.frame_task.FrameTask

methods.name: dart_physics.mink.tasks.frame_task.FrameTask.set_target
documentation:
Set the target pose.

Args:
    transform_target_to_world: Transform from the task target frame to the
        world frame.

methods.name: dart_physics.mink.tasks.frame_task.FrameTask.set_target_from_configuration
documentation:
Set the target pose from a given robot configuration.

Args:

: configuration: Robot configuration $q$.

methods.name: dart_physics.mink.tasks.frame_task.FrameTask.compute_error
documentation:
Compute the frame task error.

This error is a twist $e(q) \in se(3)$ expressed in the local frame,
i.e., it is a body twist. It is computed by taking the right-minus difference
between the target pose $T_{0t}$ and current frame pose $T_{0b}$:

$$
e(q) := {}_b \xi_{0b} = -(T_{t0} \ominus T_{b0})
= -\log(T_{t0} \cdot T_{0b}) = -\log(T_{tb}) = \log(T_{bt})
$$

where $b$ denotes our frame, $t$ the target frame and
$0$ the inertial frame.

Args:

: configuration: Robot configuration $q$.

Returns:

: Frame task error vector $e(q)$.

methods.name: dart_physics.mink.tasks.frame_task.FrameTask.compute_jacobian
documentation:
Compute the frame task Jacobian.

The derivation of the formula for this Jacobian is detailed in
[^cite_frametaskjacobian].

Args:

: configuration: Robot configuration $q$.

Returns:

: Frame task jacobian $J(q)$.

Regulate the pose of a frame expressed in the world frame.

Attributes:
    frame_name: Name of the frame to regulate, typically the name of body, geom
        or site in the robot model.
    frame_type: The frame type: `body`, `geom` or `site`.
    transform_frame_to_world: Target pose of the frame.

modules.name: dart_physics.mink.tasks.task
documentation: All kinematic tasks derive from the {class}`Task` base class.

classes.name: dart_physics.mink.tasks.task.Objective

methods.name: dart_physics.mink.tasks.task.Objective.value
documentation: Returns the value of the objective at the input vector.

class_variables.name: dart_physics.mink.tasks.task.Objective.H
documentation: Hessian matrix, of shape (n_v, n_v)

class_variables.name: dart_physics.mink.tasks.task.Objective.c
documentation: Linear vector, of shape (n_v,).
Quadratic objective of the form $\frac{1}{2} x^T H x + c^T x$.

classes.name: dart_physics.mink.tasks.task.Task

methods.name: dart_physics.mink.tasks.task.Task.__init__
documentation:
Constructor.

Args:
    cost: Cost vector with the same dimension as the error of the task.
    gain: Task gain alpha in [0, 1] for additional low-pass filtering. Defaults
        to 1.0 (no filtering) for dead-beat control.
    lm_damping: Unitless scale of the Levenberg-Marquardt (only when the error
    is large) regularization term, which helps when targets are infeasible.
    Increase this value if the task is too jerky under unfeasible targets, but
    beware that a larger damping slows down the task.

methods.name: dart_physics.mink.tasks.task.Task.compute_error
documentation:
Compute the task error at the current configuration.

Args:

: configuration: Robot configuration $q$.

Returns:

: Task error vector $e(q)$.

methods.name: dart_physics.mink.tasks.task.Task.compute_jacobian
documentation:
Compute the task Jacobian at the current configuration.

Args:

: configuration: Robot configuration $q$.

Returns:

: Task jacobian $J(q)$.

methods.name: dart_physics.mink.tasks.task.Task.compute_qp_objective
documentation:
Compute the matrix-vector pair $(H, c)$ of the QP objective.

This pair is such that the contribution of the task to the QP objective is:

$$
\| J \Delta q + \alpha e \|_{W}^2 = \frac{1}{2} \Delta q^T H
\Delta q + c^T q
$$

The weight matrix $W \in \mathbb{R}^{k \times k}$ weights and
normalizes task coordinates to the same unit. The unit of the overall
contribution is [cost]^2.

Args:

: configuration: Robot configuration $q$.

Returns:

: Pair $(H(q), c(q))$.

Abstract base class for kinematic tasks.

Subclasses must implement the configuration-dependent task error
{py:meth}`~Task.compute_error` and Jacobian {py:meth}`~Task.compute_jacobian`
functions.

The error function $e(q) \in \mathbb{R}^{k}$ is the quantity that
the task aims to drive to zero ($k$ is the dimension of the
task). It appears in the first-order task dynamics:

$$
J(q) \Delta q = -\alpha e(q)
$$

The Jacobian matrix $J(q) \in \mathbb{R}^{k \times n_v}$, with
$n_v$ the dimension of the robot's tangent space, is the
derivative of the task error $e(q)$ with respect to the
configuration $q \in \mathbb{R}^{n_q}$. The configuration displacement
$\Delta q$ is the output of inverse kinematics; we divide it by dt to get a
commanded velocity.

In the first-order task dynamics, the error $e(q)$ is multiplied
by the task gain $\alpha \in [0, 1]$. This gain can be 1.0 for
dead-beat control (*i.e.* converge as fast as possible), but might be
unstable as it neglects our first-order approximation. Lower values
cause slow down the task, similar to low-pass filtering.

modules.name: dart_physics.mink.solve_ik
documentation: Build and solve the inverse kinematics problem.

functions.name: dart_physics.mink.solve_ik.build_ik
documentation:
Build quadratic program from current configuration and tasks.

Args:
    configuration: Robot configuration.
    tasks: List of kinematic tasks.
    dt: Integration timestep in [s].
    damping: Levenberg-Marquardt damping.
    limits: List of limits to enforce. Set to empty list to disable. If None,
        defaults to a configuration limit.

Returns:
    Quadratic program of the inverse kinematics problem.

functions.name: dart_physics.mink.solve_ik.solve_ik
documentation:
Solve the differential inverse kinematics problem.

Computes a velocity tangent to the current robot configuration. The computed
velocity satisfies at (weighted) best the set of provided kinematic tasks.

Args:
    configuration: Robot configuration.
    tasks: List of kinematic tasks.
    dt: Integration timestep in [s].
    solver: Backend quadratic programming (QP) solver.
    damping: Levenberg-Marquardt damping.
    safety_break: If True, stop execution and raise an exception if
        the current configuration is outside limits. If False, print a
        warning and continue execution.
    limits: List of limits to enforce. Set to empty list to disable. If None,
        defaults to a configuration limit.
    kwargs: Keyword arguments to forward to the backend QP solver.

Returns:
    Velocity `v` in tangent space.

modules.name: dart_physics.mink.limits
documentation: Kinematic limits.

modules.name: dart_physics.mink.limits.velocity_limit
documentation: Joint velocity limit.

classes.name: dart_physics.mink.limits.velocity_limit.VelocityLimit

methods.name: dart_physics.mink.limits.velocity_limit.VelocityLimit.__init__
documentation:
Initialize velocity limits.

Args:
    model: MuJoCo model.
    velocities: Dictionary mapping joint name to maximum allowed magnitude in
        [m]/[s] for slide joints and [rad]/[s] for hinge joints.

methods.name: dart_physics.mink.limits.velocity_limit.VelocityLimit.compute_qp_inequalities
documentation:
Compute the configuration-dependent joint velocity limits.

The limits are defined as:

$$
-v_{\text{max}} \cdot dt \leq \Delta q \leq v_{\text{max}} \cdot dt
$$

where $v_{max} \in {\cal T}$ is the robot's velocity limit
vector and $\Delta q \in T_q({\cal C})$ is the displacement in the
tangent space at $q$. See the {ref}`derivations` section for
more information.

Args:

: configuration: Robot configuration $q$.
  dt: Integration timestep in [s].

Returns:

: Pair $(G, h)$ representing the inequality constraint as
  $G \Delta q \leq h$, or `None` if there is no limit.

Inequality constraint on joint velocities in a robot model.

Floating base joints are ignored.

Attributes:
    indices: Tangent indices corresponding to velocity-limited joints.
    limit: Maximum allowed velocity magnitude for velocity-limited joints, in
        [m]/[s] for slide joints and [rad]/[s] for hinge joints.
    projection_matrix: Projection from tangent space to subspace with
        velocity-limited joints.

modules.name: dart_physics.mink.limits.configuration_limit
documentation: Joint position limit.

classes.name: dart_physics.mink.limits.configuration_limit.ConfigurationLimit

methods.name: dart_physics.mink.limits.configuration_limit.ConfigurationLimit.__init__
documentation:
Initialize configuration limits.

Args:
    model: MuJoCo model.
    gain: Gain factor in (0, 1] that determines how fast each joint is
        allowed to move towards the joint limits at each timestep. Values lower
        ttan 1 are safer but may make the joints move slowly.
    min_distance_from_limits: Offset in meters (slide joints) or radians
        (hinge joints) to be added to the limits. Positive values decrease the
        range of motion, negative values increase it (i.e. negative values
        allow penetration).

methods.name: dart_physics.mink.limits.configuration_limit.ConfigurationLimit.compute_qp_inequalities
documentation:
Compute the configuration-dependent joint position limits.

The limits are defined as:

$$
{q \ominus q_{min}} \leq \Delta q \leq {q_{max} \ominus q}
$$

where $q \in {\cal C}$ is the robot's configuration and
$\Delta q \in T_q({\cal C})$ is the displacement in the tangent
space at $q$. See the {ref}`derivations` section for more information.

Args:

: configuration: Robot configuration $q$.
  dt: Integration timestep in [s].

Returns:

: Pair $(G, h)$ representing the inequality constraint as
  $G \Delta q \leq h$, or `None` if there is no limit.

Inequality constraint on joint positions in a robot model.

Floating base joints are ignored.

class_variables.name: dart_physics.mink.limits.limit.Constraint.h
documentation: Shape (nv,).

Inactive if G and h are None.

classes.name: dart_physics.mink.limits.limit.Limit

methods.name: dart_physics.mink.limits.limit.Limit.compute_qp_inequalities
documentation:
Compute limit as linearized QP inequalities of the form:

$$
G(q) \Delta q \leq h(q)
$$

where $q \in {\cal C}$ is the robot's configuration and
$\Delta q \in T_q({\cal C})$ is the displacement in the tangent
space at $q$.

Args:

: configuration: Robot configuration $q$.
  dt: Integration time step in [s].

Returns:

: Pair $(G, h)$.

Abstract base class for kinematic limits.

Subclasses must implement the {py:meth}`~Limit.compute_qp_inequalities` method
which takes in the current robot configuration and integration time step and
returns an instance of {class}`Constraint`.

documentation:
Returns true if the geoms are part of the same body, or if their bodies are

documentation: Returns true if the geom bodies have a parent-child relationship.

documentation: Returns true if the geoms pass the contype/conaffinity check.

classes.name: dart_physics.mink.limits.collision_avoidance_limit.CollisionAvoidanceLimit

methods.name: dart_physics.mink.limits.collision_avoidance_limit.CollisionAvoidanceLimit.__init__
documentation:
Initialize collision avoidance limit.

Args:
    model: MuJoCo model.
    geom_pairs: Set of collision pairs in which to perform active collision
        avoidance. A collision pair is defined as a pair of geom groups. A geom
        group is a set of geom names. For each collision pair, the mapper will
        attempt to compute joint velocities that avoid collisions between every
        geom in the first geom group with every geom in the second geom group.
        Self collision is achieved by adding a collision pair with the same
        geom group in both pair fields.
    gain: Gain factor in (0, 1] that determines how fast the geoms are
        allowed to move towards each other at each iteration. Smaller values
        are safer but may make the geoms move slower towards each other.
    minimum_distance_from_collisions: The minimum distance to leave between
        any two geoms. A negative distance allows the geoms to penetrate by
        the specified amount.
    collision_detection_distance: The distance between two geoms at which the
        active collision avoidance limit will be active. A large value will
        cause collisions to be detected early, but may incur high computational
        cost. A negative value will cause the geoms to be detected only after
        they penetrate by the specified amount.
    bound_relaxation: An offset on the upper bound of each collision avoidance
        constraint.

methods.name: dart_physics.mink.limits.collision_avoidance_limit.CollisionAvoidanceLimit._compute_contact_with_minimum_distance
documentation: Returns the smallest signed distance between a geom pair.
Private methods.

methods.name: dart_physics.mink.limits.collision_avoidance_limit.CollisionAvoidanceLimit._homogenize_geom_id_list
documentation:
Take a heterogeneous list of geoms (specified via ID or name) and return
a homogenous list of IDs (int).

methods.name: dart_physics.mink.limits.collision_avoidance_limit.CollisionAvoidanceLimit._construct_geom_id_pairs
documentation:
Returns a set of geom ID pairs for all possible geom-geom collisions.

The contacts are added based on the following heuristics:
    1) Geoms that are part of the same body or weld are not included.
    2) Geoms where the body of one geom is a parent of the body of the other
        geom are not included.
    3) Geoms that fail the contype-conaffinity check are ignored.

Note:
    1) If two bodies are kinematically welded together (no joints between them)
        they are considered to be the same body within this function.

Normal velocity limit between geom pairs.

Attributes:
    model: MuJoCo model.
    geom_pairs: Set of collision pairs in which to perform active collision
        avoidance. A collision pair is defined as a pair of geom groups. A geom
        group is a set of geom names. For each geom pair, the solver will
        attempt to compute joint velocities that avoid collisions between every
        geom in the first geom group with every geom in the second geom group.
        Self collision is achieved by adding a collision pair with the same
        geom group in both pair fields.
    gain: Gain factor in (0, 1] that determines how fast the geoms are
        allowed to move towards each other at each iteration. Smaller values
        are safer but may make the geoms move slower towards each other.
    minimum_distance_from_collisions: The minimum distance to leave between
        any two geoms. A negative distance allows the geoms to penetrate by
        the specified amount.
    collision_detection_distance: The distance between two geoms at which the
        active collision avoidance limit will be active. A large value will
        cause collisions to be detected early, but may incur high computational
        cost. A negative value will cause the geoms to be detected only after
        they penetrate by the specified amount.
    bound_relaxation: An offset on the upper bound of each collision avoidance
        constraint.

documentation: Desired period between two calls to {func}`sleep`, in seconds.

documentation:
Get the time remaining until the next expected clock tick.

Returns:

methods.name: dart_physics.utils.loop_rate_limiter.RateLimiter.sleep
documentation: Sleep for the duration required to regulate inter-call frequency.

Regulate the frequency between calls to the same instruction.

This rate limniter is meant to be used in e.g. a loop or callback function.
It is, in essence, the same as [rospy.Rate]. It assumes Python's performance
counter never jumps backward nor forward, so that it does not handle such
cases contrary to [rospy.Rate].

Attributes:

: name: Human-readable name used for logging.
  warn: If set (default), warn when the time between two calls

  > exceeded the rate clock.

[rospy.rate]: https://github.com/ros/ros_comm/blob/noetic-devel/clients/rospy/src/rospy/timer.py
